evidence_quotes.quote: Advertising companies should be regulated that they are not allowed to influence technology that prevents people from having the choice.
without a user actively noticed, he or she pays for it with his or her own data, his or her digital life.

paragraphs.text: With regard to fairness in the digital market, I would like to highlight browsers and web security. Many EU authorities, but also US authorities, recommend ad-blockers to internet users. Advertising nowadays not only makes many pages unusable, they also track user behaviour, are sometimes harmful or fraudulent, and are now increasingly harmful to young people or simply dissuading them. The use of an AdBlocker is therefore simply necessary in order not to receive Porno advertising on the S-Bahn. However, instead of regulating dangerous advertising as a responsible company, Google prefers to do so under the false claim of security (why I heard? Hust. Chat control. Hust) change the API of your browser. Google is abusing the fact that Chromium has the largest market share to guarantee its own interests, the exclusion of users for its own profit. If there is further interest, I would like to point out that UBlockOrigin is an extension, including Chromium, which has been attacked in particular as a result. It is true that Google will ensure during any hearings that it has been done purely from security aspects, but I would prefer you to listen to external security experts rather than to stakeholders. Google is both a technology company and a web company. This does not reconcile both, as they shape the technology in such a way that, without a user actively noticed, he or she pays for it with his or her own data, his or her digital life. Advertising companies should be regulated that they are not allowed to influence technology that prevents people from having the choice.
